mnemonics: Hyper-Visor Guards The Vault (H-V-G-T-V: Hypervisor, Verification, Granular Control, Trusted Computing Base, Virtualization Layer)
keyPoints: A trusted hypervisor is a security-hardened virtual machine monitor (VMM) that forms the foundation for secure cloud computing.
It provides strong isolation between guest virtual machines (VMs) to prevent one tenant from accessing another's data.
Its code is minimized and verified (often through formal methods) to reduce its attack surface and increase its reliability.
commonMistakes: Assuming a standard hypervisor offers the same level of security and isolation as a purpose-built trusted hypervisor.
Confusing the hypervisor's role with other security components like firewalls, believing the hypervisor alone is responsible for all cloud security.
